channel.title: Blue Cross VT News Mentions
channel.description: Mentions of BCBSVT, Blue Cross VT, and Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont from Vermont news outlets.

item.title: MyNBC5: Suspect dead after Texas shooting kills 1 and leaves 9 injured, police say
item.description:
Source: MyNBC5

Matched terms: Hospitals

A shooting in Texas resulted in one death and nine injuries, with those injured being taken to the hospital.

Why included: Mentions hospitals but not BCBSVT or Vermont.

Read the original story

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Vermont gets $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Health care

Vermont is receiving $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants to support projects including modernizing healthcare facilities.

Why included: Grant funding for healthcare facility modernization.

Read the original story

item.title: MyChamplainValley: Shooter, one victim dead in Texas shooting
item.description:
Source: MyChamplainValley

Matched terms: Hospitals

A shooter and one victim are dead following an active shooter situation in Midland, Texas, which ended near a veterinary hospital.

Why included: Mentions hospitals but not BCBSVT or Vermont.

Read the original story

Article body fetch warning: HTTP 403 while fetching https://www.mychamplainvalley.com/news/national-news/1-dead-11-known-victims-in-texas-shooting/

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: Major Medicare Advantage insurers appear to deny care for profit, federal watchdog finds - Healthcare Dive
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare, Health insurance, Health care

A federal watchdog report indicates that major Medicare Advantage insurers may be denying care to increase profits.

Why included: Medicare Advantage coverage and insurer practices

Read the original story

item.title: MyChamplainValley: Three injured, one seriously, in Townshend crash
item.description:
Source: MyChamplainValley

Matched terms: Vermont hospitals & providers

A car crash in Townshend resulted in three injuries, with one person airlifted to Dartmouth-Hitchcock Medical Center.

Why included: Hospital utilization and emergency services

Read the original story

Article body fetch warning: HTTP 403 while fetching https://www.mychamplainvalley.com/news/local-news/three-injured-one-seriously-in-townshend-crash/

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: ADA urges dental-specific approach in CMS interoperability, prior authorization proposal - American Dental Association
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Prior authorization & claims

The American Dental Association is requesting that CMS adopt a dental-specific approach regarding proposed interoperability and prior authorization rules.

Why included: Regulatory changes to prior authorization impact BCBSVT operations.

Read the original story

item.title: MyChamplainValley: Massive measles outbreak in Virginia county continues to grow, nearly 90 cases reported
item.description:
Source: MyChamplainValley

Matched terms: Vaccines

A significant measles outbreak in Virginia is prompting health officials to advise unvaccinated individuals to avoid large gatherings.

Why included: Public health outbreaks impact regional health care utilization.

Read the original story

Article body fetch warning: HTTP 403 while fetching https://www.mychamplainvalley.com/news/national-news/massive-measles-outbreak-in-virginia-county-continues-to-grow-nearly-90-cases-reported/

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: House committee votes to halt prior authorization pilot impacting radiology services - Radiology Business
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Prior authorization & claims

A House committee has voted to stop a pilot program that required prior authorization for certain radiology services.

Why included: Legislative scrutiny of prior authorization affects insurance practices.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: Are Medicare Advantage Plans Bad for People with Cancer? - Breast Cancer.org
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare

This article explores concerns regarding the quality of care provided by Medicare Advantage plans for patients diagnosed with cancer.

Why included: Medicare Advantage performance impacts broader health insurance reputation.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: Health Brief: Watchdog flags Medicare Advantage denials - The Washington Post
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare

A federal watchdog report has identified issues with coverage denials within Medicare Advantage plans.

Why included: Regulatory scrutiny of Medicare Advantage denials affects industry standards.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Vermont Health Search: UVM Health eliminates, restructures 142 positions - MSN
item.description:
Source: Google News Vermont Health Search

Matched terms: UVM Health

UVM Health has announced the elimination and restructuring of 142 positions within its organization.

Why included: Major provider staffing changes impact local health care access.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: Medicare Advantage Plans Often Inappropriately Deny Access to Skilled Nursing Care - Medicare Rights Center
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare

The Medicare Rights Center reports that Medicare Advantage plans frequently deny access to skilled nursing care inappropriately.

Why included: Coverage denial trends impact Medicare Advantage regulatory environment.

Read the original story

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Vermont Women’s Fund awards over $400,000 in grants to local nonprofits
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Mental health

The Vermont Women's Fund has awarded over $400,000 in grants to local nonprofits, including one for expanding mental health services.

Why included: Grant funding for mental health services.

Read the original story

item.title: Vermont Public: State regulators approve new surgery center for the Upper Valley
item.description:
Source: Vermont Public

Matched terms: Hospitals

Vermont regulators have approved a new surgery center in the Upper Valley intended to reduce wait times and costs.

Why included: New facility approvals impact regional health care costs.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: Vermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters?
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Vermont's largest health insurer

BCBSVT is proposing new, lower-cost insurance plans to address declining enrollment in the state marketplace. The insurer is currently working with the Green Mountain Care Board to develop these options.

Why included: Names BCBSVT directly and discusses product strategy.

Read the original story

item.title: Valley News: Randolph hospital offers extended-release opioid treatment in emergency department
item.description:
Source: Valley News

Matched terms: Hospitals, Opioids & addiction

Randolph hospital has introduced extended-release opioid treatment within its emergency department. This initiative aims to improve addiction care services.

Why included: Relevant to Vermont health care and addiction treatment.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: OIG: Frequent MA prior authorization denials for long-term care hospitals, inpatient rehab - Fierce Healthcare
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Hospitals, Health care, Prior authorization & claims, Senior & long-term care

The Office of Inspector General found frequent prior authorization denials for long-term care and inpatient rehabilitation in Medicare Advantage plans.

Why included: Prior authorization oversight affects insurance claims management.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Vermont Health Search: UVM Health cuts more jobs as budget strains continue - Burlington Free Press
item.description:
Source: Google News Vermont Health Search

Matched terms: UVM Health

UVM Health is implementing further job cuts as the organization continues to face significant budget pressures.

Why included: Provider financial instability impacts local health care systems.

Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

item.description:

The article examines how Medicare Advantage rebates negatively impact the market for stand-alone prescription drug plans.

Why included: Medicare Advantage market dynamics affect competitive landscape and coverage.

item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

This analysis identifies the top 15 specialty pharmacies, noting the dominance of PBM-affiliated entities.

Why included: Pharmacy benefit management trends impact drug costs and coverage.

item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medical costs & billing

The article explores the challenges and potential fixes for the No Surprises Act regarding medical billing.

Why included: Federal billing regulations impact insurer operations and member costs.

item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

An analysis indicates that Medicare Advantage plans frequently deny seniors access to necessary rehabilitation care.

Why included: Public perception of Medicare Advantage denials impacts plan reputation.

item.title: Google News Vermont Health Search: UVM Health's network searches for ways to close budget gap after impacting 142 positions - WPTZ
item.description:
Source: Google News Vermont Health Search

Matched terms: UVM Health

UVM Health Network is implementing cost-cutting measures, including the elimination of 142 positions, to address a budget deficit.

Why included: UVM Health financial stability impacts local provider networks and costs.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Vermont Health Search: More cuts could be coming at UVM Health amid statewide cost-cutting push - WCAX
item.description:
Source: Google News Vermont Health Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross

UVM Health is facing potential service cuts as part of a broader effort to manage hospital costs and lower insurance premiums. The report notes that BCBSVT premiums have risen significantly over the last five years.

Why included: Hospital cost pressure affects premiums.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: Vermont Conversation: Are smartphones birth control? Economist Caitlin Myers on sex, abortion access and talking across divides
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Reproductive health

An economist discusses how smartphone usage and digital habits may be impacting sexual health and abortion access. The study explores broader societal trends in reproductive health.

Why included: Relates to reproductive health coverage considerations.

Read the original story

item.title: WCAX: New York to end Essential Plan healthcare coverage for 450,000 residents
item.description:
Source: WCAX

Matched terms: Health insurance, Health care

New York state is ending its Essential Plan healthcare coverage for 450,000 residents by June 30. This program previously provided insurance to low-income individuals.

Why included: Regional health insurance policy changes.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: The state can’t fix Vermont’s hospitals by telling them what to do
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Hospitals

An editorial argues that state mandates are insufficient to fix Vermont's hospital system. It suggests that price reform is necessary to drive meaningful transformation.

Why included: Hospital pricing directly impacts insurance premiums.

item.title: Google News Vermont Health Search: Times Argus Reports on Leffler, Sanders Health Care Town Hall - University of Vermont
item.description:

item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont

BCBSVT has launched a marketing campaign encouraging members to seek care at independent facilities rather than large academic medical centers. The campaign is supported by a new website, VT Affordable Care.

Why included: BCBSVT strategy to manage hospital costs and utilization.

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage will not sell plans at all in 2026 - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross

Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont announced that Vermont Blue Advantage will not offer Medicare Advantage plans in 2026. This decision affects approximately 26,000 current enrollees.

Why included: Significant change to BCBSVT product offerings.

item.title: Bennington Banner: Sanders calls for investigation into vaccine committee firings

item.title: VTDigger Facebook: VTDigger Facebook post: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’
item.description:
Source: VTDigger Facebook

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Health care

Regulators are questioning whether a new suite of cheaper plans proposed by Blue Cross Blue Shield will result in actual savings.

Why included: Regulatory scrutiny of proposed BCBSVT product offerings.

Comments:

  • Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.
  • Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit
  • Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you
  • Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare
  • Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors
  • Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything
  • Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.
  • Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.
  • Beth Stevens: My plan for myself a child was supposed to be 650 a month with a 5k deductible, got my bill over 900 a month. I canceled as I was still paying for meds and co pay
  • Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up

Read the original story
